Cincinnati.description: I grew up in Cincinnati, Ohio. As a young child I would spend my days buried in books, begrudgingly practicing piano, or playing tennis. In high school I lost all 3 student government races I ran for, but I had fun in mock trial and on the tennis team.

Calgary.description: Calgary is where my mom grew up and where me and my younger sister would spend part of our summers. I fondly remember the Calgary Stampede (the world's greatest rodeo) and the accompanying pancake breakfasts. Banff and the olympic park were other consistent highlights!

Charlottesville.description: I was lucky enough to go to University of Virginia, where I received my Undergraduate and Master's degree. One of the highlights of my experience was living at 5 West Range, 4 doors down from where Edgar Allen Poe lived, wrote, and gambled as a student! As a student, I discovered my love for teaching as a TA for Intro to CS & Algorithms and also discovered the joys of reese's peanut butter cup smores.

Boston + Cambridge.description: I spent a couple summer in Cambridge, Massachussettes interning with Red Hat. It was a great chance to learn and grow! I especially loved eating at Zinnekin's Waffles and Nu Kitchen after climbing at Central Rock Climbing Gym.

Washington, D.C..description: Washington, D.C. is where I live now. I moved here during the Great Pandemic of 2020 and never actually been to my office. In my world, DC is a quiet city with light traffic and pleasant bike rides. My favorite place in DC is the National Arboretum and is my first recommendation to any visitor. Currently, I work at American Forests as the Senior Manager of Data Science.
